meta_title: For Students: Claude Code skills setup to Solve no version control (Beginner Resource Page)
meta_description: Learn how to Claude Code skills setup and overcome no version control. A Beginner-level Resource Page using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Claude Code Setup: Fix No Version Control for Students
content.seo_description: Master Claude Code skills to solve version control issues. A step-by-step beginner guide using Antigravity to build professional-grade AI applications.
content.answer_block: To solve the no-version-control trap, initialize a Git repository immediately and integrate Claude Code to automate commit messages and branch management. By using Antigravity to scaffold your project structure and Claude Code to maintain commit history, you ensure your work is tracked, safe, and ready for professional review.
content.intro:
You are staring at a folder of scattered, unsaved files—the graveyard of a thousand abandoned projects. Every line of code you write without version control is a gamble against hardware failure and chaotic logic. It’s the difference between a professional developer and an amateur hobbyist.

But this isn't just about saving files; it's about building a narrative for your career. By mastering Claude Code and robust versioning workflows, you transform your local directory into a structured, deployable asset. Let’s stop the bleeding and build your first production-ready repository.

steps.title: Initialize the Repository
steps.description: Establish a secure foundation for your project. This creates the 'source of truth' that Claude Code will track.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a standard Python project folder structure with a .gitignore file tailored for VS Code, node_modules, and virtual environments.
steps.claude_code_command: git init && git add . && git commit -m "Initial project scaffold"

steps.title: Configure Claude Code Access
steps.description: Grant Claude the necessary permissions to read your file system and execute git commands safely.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Explain the minimum required permissions for Claude Code to access local git repositories without compromising system security.

steps.title: Automate Branching Strategy
steps.description: Learn to use branches to isolate new features, preventing 'main' branch corruption.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a list of best practices for a student-level git branching strategy for a single-developer AI project.

steps.title: Semantic Commit Generation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to write professional, descriptive commit messages that document your learning journey.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze my current changes and write a professional conventional commit message detailing the logic implemented in this file.

steps.title: Synchronize with Remote Storage
steps.description: Push your local work to GitHub, ensuring your portfolio is visible and backed up.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide the terminal commands to link this local folder to a new empty GitHub repository and push the initial commit.
steps.claude_code_command: git remote add origin <url> && git push -u origin main

pro_tips: Always commit before asking Claude to refactor code; it allows you to 'undo' entire architecture changes instantly.
Use a .gitignore generator to avoid committing heavy model weights or API keys that expose you to security risks.
Tag your releases using 'git tag -a v1.0' to mark significant milestones for your portfolio reviewers.
Review Claude's generated git diffs manually; it teaches you how to read code changes at a professional level.
Run 'claude status' frequently to see if you have uncommitted changes before starting a new coding session.
content.next_steps: Once your repository is live, create a README.md file using Claude to document your project's intent, tech stack, and the specific problems you solved.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a coder; you are an engineer with a versioned, professional workflow. Your portfolio is now a living timeline of your growth. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
